message:
test(conformance): expand oracle coverage with 10+ new SQL conformance scenarios

conformance_oracle_s55.rs:
- Un-ignore test_conformance_fk_on_update_cascade_s68bo: the FK ON
  UPDATE CASCADE gap (previously applying SET NULL instead of CASCADE)
  is now fixed, so the #[ignore] annotation is removed.

New oracle-validated conformance tests (all compare fsqlite vs rusqlite):
- s69bq: FK ON DELETE CASCADE with multi-level chain (grandparent ->
  parent -> child), verifying transitive cascade propagation deletes
  all dependent rows across three levels.
- s69br: multi-column GROUP BY with HAVING and ORDER BY, covering
  SUM/COUNT/AVG/MIN/MAX aggregates across region x product dimensions.
- s69bs: UPDATE with correlated subquery (JOIN-like pattern using
  MAX(pct) from discounts table), testing COALESCE fallback for
  unmatched rows.
- s69bt: complex CASE expressions with NULL, type mixing (integer,
  real, text, blob), typeof(), NULLIF, COALESCE, and IIF.
- s69bu: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT with DO UPDATE using excluded.val,
  MAX(val, excluded.val), conditional WHERE on excluded, and
  updated_count tracking.
- s69bv: multi-table transaction with savepoints, partial rollback,
  and commit verification across interleaved DML.
- s69bw: window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG, LEAD,
  SUM OVER, NTILE) with PARTITION BY and ORDER BY.
- s69bx: recursive CTE (org chart traversal) with depth tracking,
  path concatenation, and leaf-node identification.
- s69by: compound SELECT operators (UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT,
  EXCEPT) with ORDER BY and type coercion verification.
- s69bz: date/time function coverage (date, time, datetime, strftime,
  julianday) with arithmetic modifiers and edge cases.

Each test follows the established oracle pattern: identical setup and
DML executed against both engines, then a battery of SELECT queries
compared for exact row-level equivalence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
